title: install.sh: write config.json from the binary's canonical output
action: Replace install.sh's hand-written config.json literal (the printf block around line 149-152, missing agent_presets per D1) with a call into the just-installed binary: after `install -m 0755 "$tmp_dir/$asset/herdr-go" "$BIN_DIR/herdr-go"` and before the OS-specific service setup, within the existing `if [[ ! -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]]` guard, run `"$BIN_DIR/herdr-go" --internal-print-default-config > "$CONFIG_FILE"` (or die with a clear message on nonzero exit / empty output) instead of the printf JSON. Keep the same idempotent only-if-missing guard and the same $CONFIG_FILE path. Per D6, this closes the gap where fresh installs never got agent_presets.

truths: A fresh install.sh run on a HOME with no existing config.json produces a config.json containing agent_presets
Re-running install.sh when config.json already exists leaves it untouched (idempotent guard preserved)
A nonzero exit or empty output from the CLI branch fails the install rather than writing a broken config.json

artifacts.substantive: config.json is written by capturing herdr-go --internal-print-default-config output; no hand-written JSON literal remains

prohibitions: No change to $CONFIG_FILE's path or the surrounding OS-detection/service-registration logic

verify: ! grep -q '"bind_addr": "0.0.0.0:8787"' install.sh && grep -q -- '--internal-print-default-config' install.sh && bash -n install.sh && TMPCFG=$(mktemp -d) && cargo run --quiet --bin herdr-go -- --internal-print-default-config > "$TMPCFG/config.json" && grep -q agent_presets "$TMPCFG/config.json"

trace.outcome: install.sh now writes config.json by capturing herdr-go --internal-print-default-config output instead of a hand-written printf JSON literal missing agent_presets; idempotent only-if-missing guard and nonzero/empty-output failure handling preserved.

trace.verify_output: VERIFY_PASSED (all checks passed: no hand-written bind_addr literal, --internal-print-default-config flag present, bash syntax valid, CLI output contains agent_presets)
